rawText:
The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has removed roughly a fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas output from global markets, pushing crude prices up by forty percent and European gas prices up by seventy percent in under a month. Even if a ceasefire were reached tomorrow, the physical logistics of restoring supply would take at least four months, a timeline that makes the current crisis qualitatively different from previous energy disruptions.

The bottleneck is not simply political but industrial. Gulf producers have already cut crude output by ten million barrels per day, the deepest involuntary reduction in modern history, and restarting wells requires careful pressure management to avoid damaging reservoirs. Gas infrastructure faces even steeper obstacles: drone and missile strikes have destroyed two of fourteen liquefaction units at one of the world's largest processing facilities, repairs that will take years rather than weeks. Once production restarts, insurers who cancelled war-risk coverage are unlikely to reprice quickly, and tanker fleets have repositioned to the Atlantic, meaning round trips back to the Gulf could take up to ninety days. Downstream refineries across Asia that shut entire units for lack of feedstock will need additional weeks to safely resume operations.

The countries least equipped to absorb this shock are already showing signs of strain. In Nepal, cooking gas rationing has begun. In Sri Lanka, firms have been told to close midweek to conserve fuel. Pakistan, which sources nearly ninety percent of its energy imports from the Middle East and holds less than three months of reserves, faces a widening current-account deficit compounded by falling remittances from Gulf-based workers. Egypt carries external debt obligations this year that exceed half its foreign reserves, leaving almost no fiscal room to cushion households from rising fuel and food costs. Nitrogen fertilizer, produced from natural gas, is becoming sharply more expensive, raising the prospect of record global hunger levels if the conflict persists.

Renewables offer a partial but incomplete buffer. Countries that invested heavily in solar and wind are better insulated: in one European market, gas now sets electricity prices only fifteen percent of the time, compared with nearly ninety percent in a neighboring country that pursued less clean energy. Solar generation in Pakistan grew from under one percent to ten percent in five years, saving billions in projected import costs. Yet even in optimistic clean-energy scenarios, natural gas remains essential for the periods when the sun does not shine and the wind does not blow, and affordable long-duration storage technologies remain years from commercial deployment.

The lesson emerging from this crisis is that energy security cannot be built on a single strategy. Diversified supply, strategic reserves, smarter grid design, and sustained investment in renewables all reduce vulnerability, but none eliminates it entirely.

overview.title: The Gulf Energy Shock: No Quick Fix

sentences.original: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has removed roughly a fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas output from global markets, pushing crude prices up by forty percent and European gas prices up by seventy percent in under a month.
sentences.translation: 호르무즈 해협의 봉쇄로 세계 석유 및 액화천연가스 생산량의 약 5분의 1이 글로벌 시장에서 사라졌으며, 한 달도 안 되어 원유 가격은 40%, 유럽 가스 가격은 70% 상승했다.

grammarElements.text: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz
grammarElements.label: 주어 (명사구)

grammarElements.text: roughly a fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas output
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: pushing crude prices up by forty percent and European gas prices up by seventy percent
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: The closure of the factory left hundreds without jobs.
Liquefied natural gas is transported in specially designed tankers.

expressions.usage: 예상보다 짧은 기간에 급격한 변화가 일어났음을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Event] has removed [quantity] from [market/system], pushing [indicator] up by [percentage]
patterns.explanation: 특정 사건이 시장에서 공급을 제거하고 그 결과 가격이 상승하는 인과관계를 한 문장에 담는 패턴
examples: The drought has removed thirty percent of the wheat crop from global markets, pushing flour prices up by twenty percent.
The sanctions have removed most Russian gas from European markets, pushing wholesale prices up by sixty percent.

drillQuestions.question: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has removed roughly a fifth of the world's oil and ______ natural gas output from global markets.

sentences.notes: 기사의 도입부로, 분사구문(pushing)을 사용해 원인(봉쇄)과 결과(가격 상승)를 하나의 문장에 압축한다. a fifth라는 분수 표현에 주목.

koreanAlignment.en: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz
roughly a fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas output
koreanAlignment.ko: 세계 석유 및 액화천연가스 생산량의 약 5분의 1

koreanAlignment.en: pushing crude prices up by forty percent
in under a month

sentences.original: Even if a ceasefire were reached tomorrow, the physical logistics of restoring supply would take at least four months, a timeline that makes the current crisis qualitatively different from previous energy disruptions.
sentences.translation: 설령 내일 당장 휴전이 이루어진다 하더라도 공급을 복구하는 물리적 물류에는 최소 4개월이 걸릴 것이며, 이러한 시간표는 현재 위기를 이전의 에너지 교란과 질적으로 다르게 만든다.

grammarElements.text: Even if a ceasefire were reached tomorrow
grammarElements.label: 가정법 과거 양보절

grammarElements.text: the physical logistics of restoring supply
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: would take at least four months
grammarElements.label: 동사구 (가정법 귀결절)

grammarElements.text: a timeline that makes the current crisis qualitatively different
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Both sides agreed to a temporary ceasefire to allow aid deliveries.
This recession is qualitatively different from the 2008 crisis.

expressions.usage: 단순한 정도 차이가 아니라 근본적 성격이 다름을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Even if [best-case scenario were achieved], [reality] would still take [time], a timeline that makes [situation] [adjective]
patterns.explanation: 최상의 시나리오를 가정하더라도 현실적 한계를 보여주는 가정법 패턴
examples: Even if negotiations succeeded today, rebuilding trust would take at least a decade, a timeline that makes full recovery unlikely.
Even if funding were approved tomorrow, the vaccine rollout would take at least six months, a timeline that makes herd immunity a distant goal.

drillQuestions.question: Even if a ceasefire ______ reached tomorrow, the physical logistics of restoring supply would take at least four months.

sentences.notes: 가정법 과거(were reached)와 would의 조합은 비현실적 가정을 나타낸다. '설령 ~하더라도'라는 양보의 뉘앙스가 핵심. 동격 명사구(a timeline that...)가 앞 내용을 요약하며 의미를 확장한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Even if a ceasefire were reached tomorrow
koreanAlignment.ko: 설령 내일 당장 휴전이 이루어진다 하더라도

koreanAlignment.en: the physical logistics of restoring supply
qualitatively different from previous energy disruptions

sentences.original: The bottleneck is not simply political but industrial.
sentences.translation: 병목 현상은 단순히 정치적인 것이 아니라 산업적인 것이다.

vocabulary.example: Staff shortages are the main bottleneck in the healthcare system.

expressions.usage: 문제의 본질이 표면적 원인보다 더 깊은 곳에 있음을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The [problem] is not simply [obvious cause] but [deeper cause]
patterns.explanation: 표면적 원인을 넘어 더 근본적인 원인을 제시하는 전환 패턴
examples: The crisis is not simply financial but structural.
The challenge is not simply technical but ethical.

drillQuestions.question: The bottleneck is not simply ______ but industrial.

sentences.notes: 짧고 강렬한 주제문으로, 새 문단의 핵심 논점을 선언한다. not A but B 구조가 독자의 선입견(정치적 문제)을 교정한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The bottleneck
is not simply political
but industrial

sentences.original: Gulf producers have already cut crude output by ten million barrels per day, the deepest involuntary reduction in modern history, and restarting wells requires careful pressure management to avoid damaging reservoirs.
sentences.translation: 걸프 지역 산유국들은 이미 하루 원유 생산량을 1,000만 배럴 줄였는데, 이는 근대 역사상 가장 깊은 비자발적 감산이며, 유정 재가동에는 저류층 손상을 방지하기 위한 세심한 압력 관리가 필요하다.

grammarElements.text: the deepest involuntary reduction in modern history
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구

vocabulary.example: Many workers faced involuntary redundancy during the recession.
The oil reservoir extends several kilometers beneath the seabed.

expressions.usage: 현 사태의 전례 없는 규모를 역사적 맥락에서 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Actor] has already [action] by [quantity], the deepest [noun] in [time period], and [recovery action] requires [careful process]
patterns.explanation: 피해 규모를 동격으로 강조한 뒤 복구의 어려움을 이어서 설명하는 패턴
examples: Manufacturers have already cut output by twenty percent, the steepest decline in a decade, and rebuilding capacity requires significant capital investment.
The central bank has already raised rates by three hundred basis points, the fastest tightening in forty years, and reversing course requires months of stable data.

drillQuestions.question: Restarting wells requires careful pressure management to avoid damaging ______.

sentences.notes: 동격 명사구(the deepest involuntary reduction)가 수치의 역사적 의미를 부여한다. and로 연결된 두 번째 절이 '감산은 쉽지만 재가동은 어렵다'는 비대칭을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Gulf producers have already cut crude output by ten million barrels per day
koreanAlignment.ko: 걸프 산유국들은 이미 하루 1,000만 배럴의 원유 생산을 줄였다

koreanAlignment.en: the deepest involuntary reduction in modern history
koreanAlignment.ko: 근대 역사상 가장 깊은 비자발적 감산

koreanAlignment.en: restarting wells requires careful pressure management
koreanAlignment.ko: 유정 재가동에는 세심한 압력 관리가 필요하다

pronunciationNotes: reservoir: /ˈrezərvwɑːr/ 프랑스어 기원, 마지막 음절 강세 없음

sentences.original: Gas infrastructure faces even steeper obstacles: drone and missile strikes have destroyed two of fourteen liquefaction units at one of the world's largest processing facilities, repairs that will take years rather than weeks.
sentences.translation: 가스 인프라는 훨씬 더 가파른 장애물에 직면해 있다: 드론과 미사일 공격으로 세계 최대 규모 처리 시설 중 하나에서 14개 액화 유닛 중 2개가 파괴되었으며, 수리에는 몇 주가 아니라 몇 년이 걸릴 것이다.

grammarElements.text: drone and missile strikes have destroyed two of fourteen liquefaction units
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 구체적 설명절

grammarElements.text: repairs that will take years rather than weeks
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구 (수리 기간)

vocabulary.example: The liquefaction plant converts natural gas into LNG for export.
Ageing infrastructure is a growing concern in many developed countries.

expressions.usage: 예상 기간과 실제 기간의 극적인 격차를 강조할 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 콜론을 사용해 추상적 주장을 구체적 증거로 뒷받침하는 패턴
examples: The healthcare system faces even steeper obstacles: staffing levels have dropped to half of pre-pandemic numbers.
The education sector faces even steeper obstacles: over forty percent of schools lack functioning internet connections.

drillQuestions.question: Gas infrastructure faces even steeper obstacles: drone and missile strikes have destroyed two of fourteen ______ units.

sentences.notes: 콜론(:)이 '장애물'이라는 추상적 표현을 구체적 피해 현황으로 전환한다. rather than 구조가 복구 기간의 심각성을 극적으로 대비시킨다.

koreanAlignment.en: Gas infrastructure faces even steeper obstacles
koreanAlignment.ko: 가스 인프라는 훨씬 더 가파른 장애물에 직면해 있다

koreanAlignment.en: drone and missile strikes have destroyed two of fourteen liquefaction units
koreanAlignment.ko: 드론과 미사일 공격으로 14개 액화 유닛 중 2개가 파괴되었다

koreanAlignment.en: repairs that will take years rather than weeks
koreanAlignment.ko: 몇 주가 아니라 몇 년이 걸릴 수리

sentences.original: Once production restarts, insurers who cancelled war-risk coverage are unlikely to reprice quickly, and tanker fleets have repositioned to the Atlantic, meaning round trips back to the Gulf could take up to ninety days.
sentences.translation: 생산이 재개되더라도, 전쟁 위험 보장을 취소한 보험사들이 빠르게 재가격 책정을 할 가능성은 낮으며, 유조선단은 대서양으로 재배치된 상태여서 걸프까지 왕복하는 데 최대 90일이 소요될 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: tanker fleets have repositioned to the Atlantic
grammarElements.label: 병렬절

grammarElements.text: meaning round trips back to the Gulf could take up to ninety days
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: Markets quickly repriced risk after the central bank's surprise announcement.
The company repositioned its fleet to avoid the conflict zone.

expressions.usage: 부정적 전망을 완곡하게 표현할 때 사용하는 기본 패턴

patterns.pattern: Once [recovery begins], [obstacle A] and [obstacle B], meaning [delayed outcome]
patterns.explanation: 복구가 시작되어도 여러 장애물이 지연을 야기함을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Once borders reopen, airlines will need to rehire staff and rebuild routes, meaning normal schedules could take over a year to resume.
Once the lockdown ends, factories will need to recall workers and restock materials, meaning full output could take months to restore.

drillQuestions.question: Tanker fleets have ______ to the Atlantic, meaning round trips back to the Gulf could take up to ninety days.

sentences.notes: Once절이 '생산 재개' 시점을 설정하지만, 이후 나열되는 장애물들이 회복이 결코 빠르지 않음을 보여준다. meaning 분사구문이 결론을 도출.

koreanAlignment.en: Once production restarts
insurers who cancelled war-risk coverage are unlikely to reprice quickly
koreanAlignment.ko: 전쟁 위험 보장을 취소한 보험사들이 빠르게 재가격 책정을 할 가능성은 낮다

koreanAlignment.en: tanker fleets have repositioned to the Atlantic
round trips back to the Gulf could take up to ninety days
koreanAlignment.ko: 걸프까지 왕복에 최대 90일이 소요될 수 있다

sentences.original: Downstream refineries across Asia that shut entire units for lack of feedstock will need additional weeks to safely resume operations.
sentences.translation: 원료 부족으로 전체 설비를 가동 중단한 아시아 전역의 하류 정유소들은 안전하게 운영을 재개하는 데 추가로 몇 주가 필요할 것이다.

grammarElements.text: that shut entire units for lack of feedstock
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (주어 수식)

vocabulary.example: Downstream industries are the first to feel the impact of raw material shortages.
The plant uses ethylene as its primary feedstock.

expressions.usage: 어떤 필수 요소의 결핍이 직접적 원인임을 간결하게 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Facilities] that shut [operations] for lack of [resource] will need [time] to [resume action]
patterns.explanation: 시설 폐쇄의 원인과 재가동에 필요한 시간을 하나의 문장에 담는 패턴
examples: Factories that shut production lines for lack of semiconductors will need months to rebuild inventory.
Schools that closed classrooms for lack of teachers will need a full semester to restore normal schedules.

drillQuestions.question: Downstream refineries across Asia that shut entire units for lack of ______ will need additional weeks to safely resume operations.

sentences.notes: 문단의 마지막 문장으로, 공급망의 최종 단계(하류 정유소)까지 타격이 미침을 보여준다. downstream-feedstock 쌍은 에너지 산업 필수 어휘.

koreanAlignment.en: Downstream refineries across Asia
that shut entire units for lack of feedstock
koreanAlignment.ko: 원료 부족으로 전체 설비를 가동 중단한

koreanAlignment.en: will need additional weeks to safely resume operations
koreanAlignment.ko: 안전하게 운영을 재개하는 데 추가로 몇 주가 필요할 것이다

sentences.original: The countries least equipped to absorb this shock are already showing signs of strain.
sentences.translation: 이 충격을 흡수할 준비가 가장 안 된 국가들은 이미 긴장의 징후를 보이고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The countries least equipped to absorb this shock
grammarElements.label: 주어 (과거분사 수식)

vocabulary.example: The healthcare system is showing signs of strain under the surge of patients.

expressions.usage: 가장 취약한 대상을 지목할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The [entities] least equipped to [handle challenge] are already showing signs of [negative effect]
patterns.explanation: 가장 취약한 대상이 이미 타격을 받기 시작했음을 보여주는 도입 패턴
examples: The hospitals least equipped to handle a pandemic are already showing signs of collapse.
The small businesses least equipped to absorb rising costs are already showing signs of bankruptcy.

drillQuestions.question: The countries least ______ to absorb this shock are already showing signs of strain.

sentences.notes: 새 문단의 주제문으로, 이어질 구체적 국가 사례들의 서론 역할. least equipped라는 최상급 표현이 취약성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The countries least equipped to absorb this shock
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 충격을 흡수할 준비가 가장 안 된 국가들

koreanAlignment.en: are already showing signs of strain

sentences.original: In Nepal, cooking gas rationing has begun.

vocabulary.example: Water rationing was imposed during the severe drought.

expressions.usage: 공급 부족이 심각해져 정부가 통제에 나선 상황을 간결하게 표현

patterns.explanation: 특정 국가의 위기 대응 조치를 간결하게 보고하는 뉴스 패턴
examples: In Lebanon, electricity rationing has begun.
In Venezuela, fuel rationing has begun.

drillQuestions.question: In Nepal, cooking gas ______ has begun.

sentences.notes: 극도로 짧은 문장으로, 앞 문장의 '긴장의 징후'를 구체적 사례로 보여준다. 짧은 문장이 충격적 사실을 더 효과적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: In Nepal
cooking gas rationing has begun

sentences.original: In Sri Lanka, firms have been told to close midweek to conserve fuel.
sentences.translation: 스리랑카에서는 기업들이 연료를 절약하기 위해 주중에 문을 닫으라는 통보를 받았다.

vocabulary.example: Households were urged to conserve electricity during the heatwave.
The factory will shut midweek to carry out maintenance.

expressions.usage: 정부나 상급 기관의 지시를 수동태로 전달할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: In [country], [entities] have been told to [action] to conserve [resource]
patterns.explanation: 특정 국가에서 자원 절약을 위한 강제 조치를 보도하는 패턴
examples: In South Africa, households have been told to reduce electricity use to conserve grid capacity.
In California, residents have been told to limit outdoor watering to conserve water.

drillQuestions.question: In Sri Lanka, firms have been told to close midweek to ______ fuel.

sentences.notes: 수동태(have been told)가 정부의 강제적 지시를 암시한다. 앞 문장(네팔)과 함께 짧은 사례를 연속 배치하여 위기의 광범위함을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: In Sri Lanka
firms have been told to close midweek
to conserve fuel

sentences.original: Pakistan, which sources nearly ninety percent of its energy imports from the Middle East and holds less than three months of reserves, faces a widening current-account deficit compounded by falling remittances from Gulf-based workers.
sentences.translation: 에너지 수입의 거의 90%를 중동에서 조달하고 3개월치 미만의 비축분만 보유한 파키스탄은 걸프 지역 근로자들의 송금 감소가 겹치면서 경상수지 적자가 확대되고 있다.

grammarElements.text: which sources nearly ninety percent of its energy imports from the Middle East and holds less than three months of reserves
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절 (배경 설명)

grammarElements.text: compounded by falling remittances from Gulf-based workers
grammarElements.label: 과거분사구 (추가 원인)

vocabulary.meaning: 송금, 송금액 (해외 근로자가 본국에 보내는 돈)
vocabulary.example: Remittances from overseas workers make up a significant share of the country's GDP.
The country's current-account deficit widened to five percent of GDP.

expressions.usage: 이미 나쁜 상황에 추가 악재가 더해질 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Country], which [vulnerability A] and [vulnerability B], faces [problem] compounded by [additional factor]
patterns.explanation: 국가의 구조적 취약점을 관계절로 제시한 뒤 복합적 위기를 설명하는 패턴
examples: Japan, which imports nearly all of its energy and faces a shrinking workforce, confronts rising costs compounded by a weakening yen.
Turkey, which carries heavy external debt and runs persistent trade deficits, faces inflation compounded by currency depreciation.

drillQuestions.question: Pakistan faces a widening current-account deficit ______ by falling remittances from Gulf-based workers.

sentences.notes: 비제한적 관계절(which...)이 파키스탄의 구조적 취약성을 미리 알려주어, faces 이하의 위기를 더 심각하게 느끼게 한다. compounded by는 '설상가상'의 뉘앙스.

koreanAlignment.en: Pakistan, which sources nearly ninety percent of its energy imports from the Middle East
koreanAlignment.ko: 에너지 수입의 거의 90%를 중동에서 조달하는 파키스탄

koreanAlignment.en: holds less than three months of reserves
a widening current-account deficit
compounded by falling remittances

sentences.original: Egypt carries external debt obligations this year that exceed half its foreign reserves, leaving almost no fiscal room to cushion households from rising fuel and food costs.
sentences.translation: 이집트는 올해 외채 상환 의무가 외환보유고의 절반을 초과하여, 연료 및 식품 비용 상승으로부터 가계를 보호할 재정 여력이 거의 없다.

grammarElements.text: that exceed half its foreign reserves
grammarElements.label: 관계절

grammarElements.text: leaving almost no fiscal room to cushion households from rising fuel and food costs
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: The government has little fiscal room to increase spending.
Subsidies were introduced to cushion consumers from the price shock.

expressions.usage: 분사구문으로 자원 부족의 결과를 설명할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Country] carries [financial burden] that exceeds [benchmark], leaving almost no [resource] to [protective action]
patterns.explanation: 재정 부담이 기준을 초과하여 보호 조치가 불가능함을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The municipality carries pension liabilities that exceed its annual budget, leaving almost no funds to invest in infrastructure.
The hospital carries debts that exceed its yearly revenue, leaving almost no capacity to hire additional staff.

drillQuestions.question: Egypt carries external debt obligations this year that exceed half its foreign reserves, leaving almost no ______ room to cushion households.

sentences.notes: carries는 '지고 있다'는 비유적 의미로, 무거운 부채를 짊어진 상태를 표현한다. leaving 분사구문이 부채의 결과(보호 불능)를 자연스럽게 이어준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Egypt carries external debt obligations this year
koreanAlignment.ko: 이집트는 올해 외채 상환 의무를 지고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: that exceed half its foreign reserves
leaving almost no fiscal room
to cushion households from rising fuel and food costs
koreanAlignment.ko: 연료 및 식품 비용 상승으로부터 가계를 보호할

sentences.original: Nitrogen fertilizer, produced from natural gas, is becoming sharply more expensive, raising the prospect of record global hunger levels if the conflict persists.
sentences.translation: 천연가스로 생산되는 질소 비료가 급격히 비싸지고 있으며, 분쟁이 지속될 경우 세계 기아 수준이 사상 최고치를 기록할 전망을 높이고 있다.

grammarElements.text: raising the prospect of record global hunger levels
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: Fertilizer prices have tripled since the war began.
The prospect of a recession has unnerved financial markets.

expressions.usage: 현재 추세가 지속될 경우 우려되는 결과를 전망할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Input], produced from [resource], is becoming [more costly], raising the prospect of [consequence] if [condition]
patterns.explanation: 원자재 가격 상승이 연쇄적으로 더 큰 위기를 야기하는 인과 패턴
examples: Electricity, generated largely from natural gas, is becoming prohibitively expensive, raising the prospect of factory closures if prices remain elevated.
Wheat, grown with imported fertilizer, is becoming scarce, raising the prospect of bread shortages if supply chains remain disrupted.

drillQuestions.question: Nitrogen fertilizer is becoming sharply more expensive, raising the ______ of record global hunger levels.

sentences.notes: 에너지 위기가 식량 위기로 전이되는 연쇄 효과를 보여주는 핵심 문장. produced from natural gas라는 삽입구가 가스-비료-식량의 연결고리를 명시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Nitrogen fertilizer, produced from natural gas
is becoming sharply more expensive
raising the prospect of record global hunger levels
koreanAlignment.ko: 세계 기아 수준이 사상 최고치를 기록할 전망을 높이며

koreanAlignment.en: if the conflict persists

sentences.original: Renewables offer a partial but incomplete buffer.
sentences.translation: 재생에너지는 부분적이지만 불완전한 완충 역할을 제공한다.

vocabulary.example: Savings can act as a buffer against unexpected expenses.

expressions.usage: 도움은 되지만 충분하지 않음을 균형 있게 표현할 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 대안의 한계를 인정하면서도 가치를 부정하지 않는 균형 잡힌 평가 패턴
examples: Remote work offers a partial but incomplete solution to urban congestion.
Vaccination offers a partial but incomplete shield against new variants.

drillQuestions.question: Renewables offer a partial but incomplete ______.

sentences.notes: 새 문단의 주제문으로, partial과 incomplete의 미묘한 차이(부분적으로 작동하지만 완전한 해결은 아님)를 활용한 균형 잡힌 평가.

koreanAlignment.en: Renewables
offer a partial but incomplete buffer

sentences.original: Countries that invested heavily in solar and wind are better insulated: in one European market, gas now sets electricity prices only fifteen percent of the time, compared with nearly ninety percent in a neighboring country that pursued less clean energy.
sentences.translation: 태양광과 풍력에 대규모 투자한 국가들은 더 잘 보호받고 있다: 한 유럽 시장에서 가스가 전력 가격을 결정하는 비율은 15%에 불과한 반면, 청정에너지를 덜 추구한 이웃 국가에서는 거의 90%에 달한다.

grammarElements.text: Countries that invested heavily in solar and wind
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 포함)

grammarElements.text: in one European market, gas now sets electricity prices only fifteen percent of the time
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 구체적 예시

grammarElements.text: compared with nearly ninety percent in a neighboring country
grammarElements.label: 비교 분사구

vocabulary.example: Diversified economies are better insulated from commodity price swings.
Several nations have pursued aggressive decarbonization strategies.

expressions.usage: 두 대상 간의 정량적 차이를 제시할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Entities that took action] are better insulated: [evidence with data], compared with [contrasting data]
patterns.explanation: 사전 투자의 효과를 수치 비교로 입증하는 패턴
examples: Companies that diversified early are better insulated: their revenue dropped only five percent, compared with thirty percent for rivals that relied on a single market.
Regions that built flood defenses are better insulated: damage costs fell by sixty percent, compared with a doubling in unprotected areas.

drillQuestions.question: Countries that invested heavily in solar and wind are better ______.

sentences.notes: 콜론(:) 뒤에 15% 대 90%라는 극적인 수치 대비가 재생에너지 투자의 효과를 증명한다. insulated는 원래 '단열된'이라는 물리적 의미에서 '보호받는'으로 비유 확장.

koreanAlignment.en: Countries that invested heavily in solar and wind
are better insulated
gas now sets electricity prices only fifteen percent of the time
koreanAlignment.ko: 가스가 전력 가격을 결정하는 비율은 15%에 불과하다

koreanAlignment.en: compared with nearly ninety percent in a neighboring country

sentences.original: Solar generation in Pakistan grew from under one percent to ten percent in five years, saving billions in projected import costs.
sentences.translation: 파키스탄의 태양광 발전은 5년 만에 1% 미만에서 10%로 성장하여, 예상 수입 비용에서 수십억 달러를 절감했다.

grammarElements.text: from under one percent to ten percent
grammarElements.label: 보어 (변화 범위)

grammarElements.text: saving billions in projected import costs
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: Projected costs exceeded the original budget by thirty percent.

expressions.usage: 변화의 출발점과 도달점을 명확하게 보여줄 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Metric] grew from [starting point] to [endpoint] in [time period], saving [amount] in [category]
patterns.explanation: 성장 지표와 그 경제적 효과를 한 문장에 담는 패턴
examples: Electric vehicle adoption grew from two percent to fifteen percent in three years, saving millions in fuel imports.
Broadband coverage grew from thirty percent to eighty percent in a decade, boosting GDP by an estimated four percent.

drillQuestions.question: Solar generation in Pakistan grew from under one percent to ten percent in five years, saving billions in ______ import costs.

sentences.notes: 개발도상국의 재생에너지 성공 사례를 간결하게 제시. from-to 구조가 성장 규모를, saving 분사구문이 경제적 효과를 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Solar generation in Pakistan
grew from under one percent to ten percent
saving billions in projected import costs
koreanAlignment.ko: 예상 수입 비용에서 수십억 달러를 절감하며

sentences.original: Yet even in optimistic clean-energy scenarios, natural gas remains essential for the periods when the sun does not shine and the wind does not blow, and affordable long-duration storage technologies remain years from commercial deployment.
sentences.translation: 그러나 낙관적인 청정에너지 시나리오에서도 태양이 비추지 않고 바람이 불지 않는 시간대에는 천연가스가 여전히 필수적이며, 저렴한 장기 에너지 저장 기술의 상용화는 아직 수년이 남아 있다.

grammarElements.text: for the periods when the sun does not shine and the wind does not blow
grammarElements.label: 시간 관계절

vocabulary.example: The deployment of 5G networks is proceeding faster than expected.
Long-duration energy storage is critical for a fully renewable grid.

expressions.usage: 기술이나 목표의 실현이 아직 멀었음을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Yet even in [best-case scenario], [essential element] remains [necessary], and [solution] remains [far from reality]
patterns.explanation: 최선의 시나리오에서도 한계가 존재함을 인정하는 양보-반전 패턴
examples: Yet even in aggressive electrification scenarios, liquid fuels remain essential for aviation, and sustainable alternatives remain decades from widespread adoption.
Yet even in the most favorable economic forecasts, inequality remains a challenge, and redistributive policies remain politically difficult.

drillQuestions.question: Affordable long-duration storage technologies remain years from commercial ______.

sentences.notes: Yet으로 시작하여 앞의 낙관적 사례들에 제동을 건다. 'the sun does not shine and the wind does not blow'라는 표현은 재생에너지의 본질적 한계를 시적으로 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: Yet even in optimistic clean-energy scenarios
natural gas remains essential
when the sun does not shine and the wind does not blow
koreanAlignment.ko: 태양이 비추지 않고 바람이 불지 않을 때

koreanAlignment.en: affordable long-duration storage technologies remain years from commercial deployment
koreanAlignment.ko: 저렴한 장기 저장 기술의 상용화는 아직 수년이 남아 있다

sentences.original: The lesson emerging from this crisis is that energy security cannot be built on a single strategy.
sentences.translation: 이 위기에서 도출되는 교훈은 에너지 안보가 단일 전략 위에 구축될 수 없다는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: The lesson emerging from this crisis
grammarElements.label: 주어 (현재분사 수식)

grammarElements.text: that energy security cannot be built on a single strategy
grammarElements.label: 보어 (that 명사절)

vocabulary.example: An emerging consensus suggests that the policy has failed.

expressions.usage: 기반이 되는 전제가 불충분함을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The lesson emerging from [event] is that [principle]
patterns.explanation: 구체적 사건에서 보편적 원칙을 도출하는 결론 패턴
examples: The lesson emerging from the pandemic is that healthcare investment must be proactive, not reactive.
The lesson emerging from the financial crisis is that regulation cannot keep pace with innovation.

drillQuestions.question: The lesson emerging from this crisis is that energy security cannot be built on a ______ strategy.

sentences.notes: 마지막 문단의 도입부로, 기사 전체의 핵심 교훈을 한 문장으로 압축한다. emerging 현재분사가 교훈이 위기 속에서 실시간으로 드러나고 있음을 암시.

koreanAlignment.en: The lesson emerging from this crisis
energy security cannot be built on a single strategy
koreanAlignment.ko: 에너지 안보는 단일 전략 위에 구축될 수 없다

sentences.original: Diversified supply, strategic reserves, smarter grid design, and sustained investment in renewables all reduce vulnerability, but none eliminates it entirely.
sentences.translation: 다각화된 공급, 전략적 비축, 더 스마트한 전력망 설계, 그리고 재생에너지에 대한 지속적 투자 모두 취약성을 줄이지만, 어느 것도 완전히 제거하지는 못한다.

grammarElements.text: Diversified supply, strategic reserves, smarter grid design, and sustained investment in renewables
grammarElements.label: 주어 (병렬 4항목)

vocabulary.example: The report highlighted the vulnerability of coastal cities to flooding.
Economic recovery requires sustained investment over many years.

expressions.usage: 만능 해결책이 없음을 인정하는 결론에서 자주 쓰이는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Solution A], [B], [C], and [D] all reduce [problem], but none eliminates it entirely
patterns.explanation: 복수의 해결책이 모두 도움이 되지만 완전한 해결은 불가능하다는 겸손한 결론 패턴
examples: Better policing, education reform, social programs, and economic opportunity all reduce crime, but none eliminates it entirely.
Vaccines, masks, distancing, and ventilation all reduce transmission, but none eliminates it entirely.

drillQuestions.question: Diversified supply, strategic reserves, smarter grid design, and sustained investment in renewables all reduce ______, but none eliminates it entirely.

sentences.notes: 기사의 최종 결론으로, 4가지 해결책을 병렬 나열한 뒤 but으로 한계를 인정한다. all-but none 대비가 현실적이면서도 행동을 촉구하는 메시지를 전달.

koreanAlignment.en: Diversified supply, strategic reserves, smarter grid design, and sustained investment in renewables
koreanAlignment.ko: 다각화된 공급, 전략적 비축, 더 스마트한 전력망 설계, 그리고 재생에너지에 대한 지속적 투자

koreanAlignment.en: all reduce vulnerability
but none eliminates it entirely
koreanAlignment.ko: 하지만 어느 것도 완전히 제거하지는 못한다
